data.content:
Use `dotnet watch test` — it watches for file changes and automatically re-runs tests without a full manual rebuild:

```bash
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

This will re-run only that specific test every time you save `PaymentProcessorTests.cs`.

**The fix** is on line 29 — the validation only checks `== 0` but not negative amounts. Change:

```csharp
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
```

to:

```csharp
if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");
```

Want me to apply the fix and start the watch loop so you can see it pass?
data.reasoningText: The user wants the fastest workflow to iterate on the fix. The best approach for .NET is `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests when files change. Let me also filter to just the failing test for even faster feedback.
